What We Teach

Skills That Shape
Digital Futures

A structured curriculum that takes children from computer basics all the way to coding and real-world problem solving.

Computer Basics

Hardware, software, file management, and safe digital habits — the essential foundation for every learner.

Microsoft Word

Typing, formatting, and editing documents — essential writing and presentation skills for school assignments.

Excel & PowerPoint

Data organisation, spreadsheet formulas, and professional presentations to communicate ideas clearly.

Internet Skills

Safe browsing, digital research, email communication, and responsible online citizenship.

Typing Skills

Touch typing techniques and keyboard confidence — building speed, accuracy, and efficiency for life.

Coding & Problem Solving

Logic, algorithms, Scratch programming, and real-world project challenges that build creative thinking.

Isiphiwo Primary School
Why Choose Us

Technology Education
Done Right

We believe every child deserves quality tech education — regardless of background or circumstance.

  • Structured Learning Levels

    Three age-appropriate levels ensuring every learner is challenged at exactly the right depth and pace.

  • 100% Hands-On Learning

    Every session is spent on real computers. Learning by doing is at the heart of everything we teach.

  • Equal Access to Technology

    We serve learners from all backgrounds, ensuring no child is left behind in the digital age.

  • Future-Ready Curriculum

    Skills aligned with what actually matters in careers, higher education, and everyday digital life.

Official Partnership

In Collaboration with Iphupha Lam

Wills Tech Computer Classes proudly operates as part of the Iphupha Lam Social Welfare After School Programme — a community-driven initiative dedicated to uplifting children through education, care, and opportunity. Together, we are building digital futures one child at a time.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

How old does my child need to be to enroll?
+

We welcome learners from ages 7 to 16. Level 1 is for ages 7–10, Level 2 for ages 10–13, and Level 3 for ages 13–16. We will assess and place your child in the most suitable level during the enrollment process.

Where are the classes held?
+

All classes are held at Isiphiwo Primary School as part of the Iphupha Lam After School Programme. We use a fully equipped computer lab with desks, chairs, and proper machines for all learners.

Does my child need any prior computer experience?
+

Not at all. Level 1 is designed for complete beginners — we start from zero and build up step by step. Every learner is welcomed at their current level and supported throughout their journey.

How do I enroll my child?
+

Fill in the Enrollment Form on this website, or contact us directly on WhatsApp at 067 120 0778. We will confirm your child's place and share all class details within 24 hours.

What must my child bring to class?
+

Nothing! All computers, learning materials, and stationery are provided. Learners just need to arrive ready to engage, learn, and have fun with technology.

What is the Iphupha Lam After School Programme?
+

Iphupha Lam Social Welfare is a community after school programme that supports children through education, nutrition, and holistic development. Wills Tech Computer Classes operates in official partnership with Iphupha Lam, bringing technology education directly into their after school environment.

Our Story

About Wills Tech

A passionate initiative bringing quality technology education to every child, regardless of background — rooted in community, driven by purpose.

Who We Are

Technology Education
for Every Child

Wills Tech Computer Classes was founded on one belief: every South African child deserves access to quality technology education — regardless of where they live, how much their family earns, or what resources their school has.

Hosted at Isiphiwo Primary School, we run a structured, hands-on programme for children aged 7 to 16. Our computer lab is a safe, welcoming space where young minds discover the exciting world of computers, coding, and digital skills. We don't just teach technology — we build confidence, curiosity, and the ability to create.

Every session is taught by an educator who understands the needs and realities of South African learners. We meet children where they are and take them somewhere remarkable.

Official Collaboration

Wills Tech operates in partnership with the Iphupha Lam Social Welfare After School Programme. Iphupha Lam provides a caring, supportive environment for children from the community — and Wills Tech brings world-class computer and technology education directly into that space. Together, we give children not just skills, but a future to dream about.

View Our Programs Enroll Your Child

Our Mission

To equip every child with the digital literacy, coding foundations, and technological confidence they need to thrive — making excellence in tech education accessible to all.

Our Vision

A future where every South African child participates in the digital economy as a confident creator, innovator, and problem-solver — not just a consumer of technology.

Our Values

Inclusion, excellence, curiosity, and community. We believe in meeting every learner where they are, building them up with patience, care, and an uncompromising standard of teaching.

Community Partnership

Through our collaboration with Iphupha Lam Social Welfare, we embed technology education directly inside a trusted community programme — removing barriers and reaching those who need it most.

Our Curriculum

Programs & Courses

Three structured levels — each designed for a specific age group, building skills progressively from computer basics all the way to coding.

Level 1

Computer Basics

Ages 7 – 10 Years
  • Introduction to computers, hardware & software
  • Mouse & keyboard fundamentals
  • Touch typing practice & speed building
  • Microsoft Word — documents & formatting
  • File management & folder organisation
  • Digital safety & responsible use
Enroll in Level 1 →
Level 2

Productivity Skills

Ages 10 – 13 Years
  • Microsoft Excel — spreadsheets & formulas
  • PowerPoint — slide design & delivery
  • Internet navigation & safe browsing
  • Email communication & online etiquette
  • Digital research & evaluation of sources
  • Introduction to cloud tools & storage
Enroll in Level 2 →
Level 3

Future Skills

Ages 13 – 16 Years
  • Coding basics — logic, variables & algorithms
  • Scratch programming — interactive projects
  • Problem-solving & computational thinking
  • Digital creativity & design thinking
  • Real-world application projects
  • Introduction to web & app development concepts
Enroll in Level 3 →
